content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 16, 1991

Bob Connelly
Chief Administrative Officer
California Legislature
Assembly Rules Committee
State Capitol, Room 3016
P. O. Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0001

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-91-123

Dear Mr. Connelly:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
by Scott Hallabrin to Barbara Milman of your office on February
20, 1991 regarding the "personal use" provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Your request does not identify a
specific person to whom our advice would apply. Accordingly, we
are providing information pursuant to the provisions of
Regulation 18329(b)(2)(A) (copy enclosed).²

Mr. Hallabrin advised Ms. Milman that the "personal use"
provisions of the Act (Sections 89511 through 89519) permit
assemblymembers to use campaign funds for the expenditures
discussed below including expenditures for the purchase or the lease
of equipment for their legislative offices. However, legal title
to such equipment must not reside, in whole or in part, in a
candidate, elected officer, member of his or her immediate family,
or the treasurer of the committee. (Section 89517.)

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-91-123
Page 2


We stress that this advice was given with the understanding
that the items leased or purchased would be used for a political,
legislative or governmental purpose. Any personal use of campaign
funds is strictly limited and permissible only when the personal
use is incidental to their use for political, legislative or
governmental purposes.

Ms. Milman was advised as follows:

1. Telephone equipment

The Act provides that campaign funds may be used for
expenditures associated with holding office if the expenditures
are reasonably related to a legislative or governmental purpose.
Expenditures which confer a substantial benefit must be
directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose. (Section 89512.) Accordingly, campaign funds may be
used to purchase or lease facsimile machines, telephone answering
machines, private telephone lines and telephones, including
cellular telephones for the assemblymembers' legislative offices
since such expenditure is reasonably related to legislative or
governmental purpose. The cellular telephones may be used in
automobiles owned by the Legislature. These items may be used by
legislators and their staff provided their use is for political,
legislative, or governmental purposes.

2. Office furniture and supplies

The Act provides that campaign funds may be used to purchase,
lease or refurbish appliances or equipment. Such items are
considered to be directly related to a political, legislative or
governmental purpose so long as their use for other purposes is
only incidental to their use for political, legislative or
governmental purposes. Title to such items must remain with the
committee. (Section 89517.) Accordingly, campaign funds may be
used to purchase office furniture, clocks, copiers, printers,
televisions, VCRs, radios, and decorations such as plants and
pictures for the assemblymembers' legislative offices. Campaign
funds may also be used to purchase small office supplies such as
staplers and sorters, as well as to pay for books, subscriptions
and clipping services. We stress, however, that this is a
permissible expenditure of campaign funds only if title to the
items remains in the committee and the items are used for a
political, legislative, or governmental purpose.

Ms. Milman also asked whether campaign funds may be used to
purchase coffee makers, microwaves, refrigerators and bottled
water. As stated above, a committee may use campaign funds to
purchase equipment such as coffee makers, microwaves, and
refrigerators so long as title to these items remains in the
committee. Purchase of such equipment will serve a legislative or
governmental purpose because the benefit will flow to the State of
California as an employer. This type of equipment is commonly

Our File No. I-91-123
Page 3


found in offices and employers commonly provide the benefit of the
use of such equipment to its employees.

     We stress, however, that the use of campaign funds to
purchase bottled water, food items and coffee is more limited.
The Act provides that campaign funds may not be used to make
personal gifts unless the gift is directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose.  Gifts with a total
cumulative value of less than $250 in a single year made to an
individual employee, a campaign committee worker, or an employee
of the elected officer's agency are considered to be directly
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.
(Section 89513(f).)  Accordingly, it is permissible to use
campaign funds to purchase bottled water, food items and coffee
for employees of the Legislature so long as the cost of the items
does not exceed $250 for each employee in each fiscal year.

3.  Gifts

     As stated above, the use of campaign funds to make gifts is
strictly limited to expenditures which are directly related to a
political, legislative or governmental purpose.  (Section
89513(f)(1).)  Therefore, photographs, resolutions and memorabilia
may be purchased with campaign funds to be given to constituents
only if the gift will be directly related to a political,
legislative or governmental purpose.

4.  Travel

     Campaign funds may be used to pay for travel when such
expenditure is directly related to a political, legislative or
governmental purpose.  (Section 89513.)  If the travel expenditure
meets standards similar to those of §§162 and 274 of the
Internal Revenue Code for deductions of travel expenses under
federal income tax law, then the expenditure is considered to be
directly related to a political, legislative or governmental
purpose.  (Section 89513(a)(1).)  Such an expenditure would be a
permissible use of campaign funds for purposes of the Act.

5.  Professional services

     Campaign funds may be used to pay for professional services
when such services are directly related to a political,
legislative or governmental purpose.  (Section 89513(b).)
Therefore, the committee may use campaign funds to pay for
professional training of the candidate and committee staff and for
attendance at conferences and courses if such professional
training or attendance at conferences and courses is directly
related to a political, legislative, or governmental purpose.

Our File No. I-91-123
Page 4


We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:dg

Enclosure
